page: Amazon Web Services x2iezn.12xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 48 dedicated vCPUs, derived from 24 physical cores, utilizing an x86_64 Intel Xeon 8252C processor with a high base clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The instance is equipped with 1536.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, yielding a resource density of 32.0 GB of RAM per core. This configuration lacks local storage, relying entirely on network-attached storage, but provides a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps.

Benchmark data highlights strong memory performance, with uncached large-block memory read bandwidth reaching 209.78 GB/sec and write bandwidth at 98.02 GB/sec, both placing in the top 25% tier. Single-threaded gzip compression is also strong at 31.81 GB/sec, alongside strong software compilation performance and strong Passmark extended CPU instructions. However, multi-core CPU performance, single-core stress-ng, and database operations score in the average tier.

Tradeoffs are evident in LLM inference workloads, where text generation speeds are weak, registering at 46.23 tokens/sec for a small 135M model and dropping to 7.82 tokens/sec for a medium 7B model. Given the high memory-to-vCPU ratio and high-frequency Intel processor, this instance offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound enterprise applications, large-scale in-memory databases, and heavy electronic design automation workloads, rather than parallelized GPU-accelerated tasks or local-storage-heavy operations.
description: Amazon Web Services x2iezn.12xlarge is a memory-optimized instance running on the Nitro hypervisor. It features 48 dedicated vCPUs from a 4.5 GHz Intel Xeon 8252C processor and 1536.0 GB of DDR4 memory, providing 32.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance lacks local storage but features a 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark results show strong large-block memory read and write bandwidth alongside strong software compilation performance. However, multi-core CPU performance and database operations remain in the average tier, and LLM text generation speeds are weak. This hardware profile is designed for memory-intensive enterprise workloads, in-memory databases, and electronic design automation applications requiring high memory capacity and high single-core clock speeds.
og_description: Amazon Web Services x2iezn.12xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 48 vCPUs, a 4.5 GHz Intel Xeon processor, 1.5 TB of DDR4 RAM, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth for memory-intensive workloads.

tagline: A memory-optimized x86 platform combining high-frequency Intel Xeon processors with 1.5 TB of RAM and 100 Gbps network bandwidth.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated vCPUs powered by a 4.5 GHz Intel Xeon 8252C processor
1536.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing 32.0 GB of RAM per physical core
100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with Nitro hypervisor integration
Strong large-block memory read and write bandwidth performance
Suited for in-memory databases, EDA, and memory-intensive enterprise applications
Lacks local storage and exhibits weak performance in LLM text generation
